content.full_text:
CHAMBERS OF
THE MUNICIPAL COURT
EAST LOS ANGELES JUDICIAL DISTRICT
4837 EAST THIRD STREET
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90022
MARIO P. GONZALEZ, JUDGE

1/12/82

Mr. Martin Aguilar
c/o Fair Political Practices Commission
1100 K Street Building
Sacramento, Calif. 95814

Dear Martin:

Trust that you had a terrific old year and that the new one
will even be better for you and yours.

Would appreciate you answering the following question for me;
in 1978, I ran for Superior Court and collected some money for my
campaign. Though I lost, I still have several thousand dollars
left in my "Committee to Elect Gonzalez to the Superior Court."

[#]Keeping the money in a savings account for my next election,
however, in the meantime, the account earns interest on a yearly
basis. This year the account earned $119.32. My question is—am I
supposed to be filing any forms with the Political Practice
Commission because of this earned income into my campaign committee
account.

Best personal regards.

[Handwritten text:]
Telephone Response:
Receipt & interest on
campaign funds does not
trigger statement. If no
contributions rec'd or
expenditures made, no
reports required.
Jeanne Hitchcock

Sincerely,

Mario Gonzalez
Judge

sections.question: In 1978, I ran for Superior Court and collected some money for my campaign. Though I lost, I still have several thousand dollars left in my "Committee to Elect Gonzalez to the Superior Court." I am keeping the money in a savings account for my next election, however, in the meantime, the account earns interest on a yearly basis. This year the account earned $119.32. My question is---am I supposed to be filing any forms with the Political Practice Commission because of this earned income into my campaign committee account.
sections.conclusion: Receipt of interest on campaign funds does not trigger statement. If no contributions rec'd or expenditures made, no reports required.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a candidate file reports with the Fair Political Practices Commission when a dormant campaign committee account earns interest income between election cycles?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: Interest earned on campaign funds held in a dormant account does not require filing reports with the FPPC if there are no contributions received or expenditures made.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document is presented as a telephone response advice letter. Question and conclusion sections are clearly demarcated in the source document. Respondent identified as Jeanne Pritchard. No formal section headers but content structure is evident from context.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: In 1978, I ran for Superior Court and collected some money for my campaign. Though I lost, I still have several thousand dollars left in my "Committee to Elect Gonzalez to the Superior Court." I am keeping the money in a savings account for my next election, however, in the meantime, the account earns interest on a yearly basis. This year the account earned $119.32. My question is---am I supposed to be filing any forms with the Political Practice Commission because of this earned income into my campaign committee account.

CONCLUSION: Receipt of interest on campaign funds does not trigger statement. If no contributions rec'd or expenditures made, no reports required.

embedding.summary: A candidate who held funds from a prior unsuccessful 1978 Superior Court campaign inquiry sought advice on whether interest earned on those dormant campaign funds required FPPC reporting. FPPC responded that interest income alone does not trigger reporting requirements absent contributions or expenditures.
